quiz.explanation: Alone means without others, by oneself.
Apartment means a set of rooms for living in a building.
Article means a piece of writing published in a newspaper or magazine.
Artist means a person who creates paintings, music, or other art.
Attitude means a way of thinking or feeling about something.
Compare means to look at similarities and differences between things.
Judge means to form an opinion or make a decision about someone.
Magazine means a publication with articles and pictures released regularly.
Material means the substance from which something is made.
Meal means food eaten at a particular time of the day.
Method means a particular way of doing something.
Neighbor means a person who lives near you.
Professional means having or showing the skill of a trained expert.
Profit means money earned after all costs are paid.
Quality means the standard or degree of excellence of something.
Shape means the outer form or appearance of something.
Space means an empty area or the region beyond Earth's atmosphere.

opts: a step to go up or down
quiz.explanation: Stair means one of a series of steps for going between floors.
Symbol means a sign, shape, or object used to represent something.

opts: having little width or small body
quiz.explanation: Thin means having little thickness or a slender body.

story.title: The Artist in the Apartment
story.body: Rina was a professional artist who lived alone in a small apartment on the fourth floor. Every morning, she climbed the stairs slowly, carrying her art materials. She never used the elevator because she believed the stairs helped her stay healthy and thin. Her apartment had a unique shape — long and narrow — but she used every inch of space wisely. On the walls, she hung symbols of her culture: a painting of a lotus, the symbol of purity, and a dove, the symbol of peace. Her neighbor, an old man named Karim, often brought her a warm meal when she forgot to eat while painting. One day, a famous magazine editor read an article about Rina's work and visited her apartment. The editor compared her paintings with those of famous artists and was amazed by their quality. He asked Rina about her method. She explained that her attitude toward life — staying calm, thinking deeply, and never judging people harshly — was the key to her art. She did not compare herself with others; she only focused on improving her own work. The editor offered to publish her artwork. The profit from the deal helped Rina buy better art material. From that day, Rina's name became a symbol of hard work and creativity in her community.

comprehension.q: What did her neighbor bring her?
What was Rina's key to great art?
What did the profit help Rina buy?

listening.title: A Conversation at the Art Exhibition
listening.script:
A: Excuse me, are you the artist who painted these?
B: Yes, I am. I live alone and paint every day.
A: Your work is amazing. What material do you use?
B: I use only high quality cotton canvas and natural colors.
A: How do you find space to work in a small apartment?
B: I arrange my tools carefully and use every corner.
A: I read an article about you in a magazine last week.
B: Yes, the editor compared my work with professional artists.
A: What is your method of painting?
B: I study shapes, symbols, and the thin lines in nature.
A: Do you ever judge your own work?
B: Yes, but with a positive attitude. I improve every day.
A: Does your neighbor disturb you?
B: No, he is kind. He brings me a meal when I forget to eat.
A: Have you made a profit from your art?
B: Yes, it helped me buy better material.
A: You are truly a professional artist.
B: Thank you. Hard work and quality never fail.

comprehension.q: What material does the artist use?
How does she arrange her apartment?
What does her neighbor do for her?
What helped her buy better material?

speaking.topic: Describing Your Living Space and Daily Life
speaking.prompt: Talk about where you live and how you spend your day. Use words like apartment, space, neighbor, meal, method, and quality.
speaking.example: I live in a small apartment with my family. Our space is limited, but we keep it organized. My neighbor is very friendly and kind. Every morning, we prepare a healthy meal together. My method of studying is simple: I read, write, and practice daily. I always try to maintain quality in my work and keep a positive attitude toward life.
tips: Mention how you interact with neighbors
Use adjectives like thin, small, high quality
Share your personal method or habit
